svg.text: MODERN HEBREW
GRAMMAR — REVIVED SEMITIC STRUCTURE
MODERN HEBREW WORD ORDER: SVO
Biblical Hebrew: VSO • Modern Hebrew: SVO (major shift)
Dan akhel et ha-tapuach. "Dan ate the apple." (SVO)
Et: accusative marker; no case on nouns themselves
SVO shift: European language influence during revival (Yiddish, Russian)
MODERN HEBREW VERB SYSTEM
Seven binyanim preserved: Qal, Nifal, Piel, Pual, Hitpael, Hifil, Hofal
New tenses: present (participle), past (perfect), future (imperfect)
Hu katav "he wrote" (past) • hu kotev "he writes" (present)
Hu yichtov "he will write" (future) • clear tense not aspect system
ROOT PRODUCTIVITY IN MODERN HEBREW
Roots still generate new words: R-D-Y (to upload/download)
lehoriid "to download" • lehaalit "to upload" (from root meanings)
kiduud "formatting" (computer) • based on traditional root patterns
ARABIC GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
ARABIC WORD ORDER: VSO (MSA) / SVO (DIALECTS)
MSA: VSO maintained • colloquial dialects: SVO predominates
Egyptian: mostly SVO • Levantine: SVO • MSA: VSO
Modern Hebrew SVO = dialects SVO (same modernization direction)
ARABIC ROOT PRODUCTIVITY
Arabic root system also generates modern terms:
hatif (phone): from H-T-F "to snatch/call out" root
hasib (computer): from H-S-B "to count/calculate" (same as Hebrew!)
Both languages: same root-based strategy for new vocabulary
GRAMMAR IN MODERN CONTACT
Israeli Arabic: Hebrew loanwords in grammar • morphological calques
Modern Hebrew: Arabic loanwords fill colloquial register
Bilingual Israelis: grammatical code-switching documented linguistically
Two ancient grammars, living side by side, still cross-pollinating
PLATE 12c ALT · MODERN HEBREW / ARABIC · GRAMMAR
